name: geeky-orchestration
description: Machine-readable index of the geeky-orchestration deterministic quality gates. Any orchestrator (CLI hook, git hook, SDK guardrail, MCP wrapper) can discover and invoke these. Every validator follows the same contract: arguments in, exit 0 = pass / exit 1 = fail, human summary on stdout, optional --json/-Json for a machine-readable object. Scripts ship as paired Python and PowerShell with identical output and exit codes. Paths are relative to the plugin root (CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT in Claude Code).

contract.stdout: human-readable summary; machine-readable JSON when --json/-Json is passed
contract.parity: the .py and .ps1 mirror of each tool produce identical output and exit codes

mcp.description: Wraps the validators as MCP tools so any MCP-capable agent can invoke them with no per-framework hook config. Thin adapter: each tool shells out to the same script and returns its --json report.
mcp.run: uv run --with mcp python mcp/server.py   (or: pip install mcp && python mcp/server.py)

validators.purpose: Verify a geeky-plan folder has the artifacts geeky-implement needs.
Each tasks/Tx-*.md carries the required template sections.
validators.when: end of geeky-plan; start of geeky-implement (plan->implement boundary)

validators.purpose: Kanban integrity: every task in exactly one lane, no dangling refs, WIP cap, lane coverage.
validators.when: after every lane move in geeky-implement; in geeky-status

validators.purpose: Definition-of-Done for one task: notes file exists, task in Done lane, handoff mentions it; prints the task's validation block to re-run.
validators.when: before moving a task to Done in geeky-implement

validators.purpose: Commit message is Conventional Commits + references a task (Tasks: T<n>).
validators.when: before each commit in geeky-implement; ideal as a git commit-msg hook
validators.args: FILE (git commit-msg path)  OR  --message <text>  OR  stdin

validators.purpose: PreToolUse-style guard: warn or block edits to frozen planning artifacts. Reads tool-call JSON on stdin.
validators.when: wired as a Claude Code PreToolUse hook (hooks/hooks.json); other CLI agents can add an equivalent pre-edit hook
